section.heading: Transfer of a prisoner to State authority
section.chapeau: The Director of the Bureau of Prisons shall order that a prisoner who has been charged in an indictment or information with, or convicted of, a State felony, be transferred to an official detention facility within such State prior to his release from a Federal prison facility if—

paragraph.content: the transfer has been requested by the Governor or other executive authority of the State;
the State has presented to the Director a certified copy of the indictment, information, or judgment of conviction; and
the Director finds that the transfer would be in the public interest.

section.continuation: If more than one request is presented with respect to a prisoner, the Director shall determine which request should receive preference. The expenses of such transfer shall be borne by the State requesting the transfer.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: For a prior section 3623, applicable to offenses committed prior to Nov. 1, 1987, see note set out preceding section 3601 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Nov. 1, 1987, and applicable only to offenses committed after the taking effect of this section, see section 235(a)(1) of Pub. L. 98–473, set out as a note under section 3551 of this title.
